claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements scoped evidence separation where WebFetch/WebSearch results are marked as external research and cannot satisfy existing-project fact requirements without project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classifies evidence by checking record.scope, record.path, record.url, record.source fields against known patterns for project/external/user/unknown
evidence.why: Primary scope classification logic that separates external research from project facts

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when summary.has_project is false (no project-scoped evidence)
evidence.why: Core validation that blocks external-only evidence from satisfying project fact requirements

evidence.summary: PRD readiness check loops through requiredRoles evidence results and calls projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() for each complete result
evidence.why: Integration point where external-only evidence becomes a PRD readiness blocker

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rule: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use WebFetch/WebSearch when available and record those records as scope=external'
evidence.why: Contract requiring agents to mark WebFetch/WebSearch results as external scope

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rule: 'WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts'
evidence.why: Explicit prohibition against using external research as project facts

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes research/fetch/web/web-research/external-research profiles to 'research' for Claude provider
evidence.why: Provider configuration that enables WebFetch/WebSearch while maintaining boundary rules

evidence.summary: Documentation defines three evidence agent protocols: explorer (read-only), cross-checker (independent verification), verifier (evidence adequacy check)
evidence.why: Product specification for evidence agent roles and separation of concerns

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states: external evidence can only be background research and cannot independently prove target project has fields/APIs/state machines/data flows
evidence.why: Product requirement that external evidence cannot prove project implementation facts

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult() checks evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() and blocks with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED when scope is missing
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement that scope must be declared on all evidence records

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error code during normalization
evidence.why: Test coverage ensuring scope declaration is enforced at runtime

assumptions: WebFetch/WebSearch tools are available in Claude provider environment when agent_tool_profile is 'research'
Agent providers respect the agent prompt contract and mark WebFetch/WebSearch results as scope=external
The boundary snapshot mechanism correctly detects project mutations outside allowed artifact roots
risks: Agent providers could ignore scope declaration rules and treat external research as project facts
WebFetch/WebSearch tools might be unavailable despite 'research' profile, causing agents to report missing instead of using external research
Boundary audit could have false positives/negatives, incorrectly blocking allowed mutations or missing actual project mutations

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all explorer evidence claims. The codebase implements complete evidence scope separation: classification logic (project/external/user/unknown), blocking validation that rejects external-only evidence for project facts, agent prompt contracts requiring WebFetch/WebSearch to be marked external, doctrine explicitly prohibiting external evidence for project facts, runtime scope enforcement, and test coverage. No contradictions found during cross-check.
